source_jobs.title: Machine Learning Research Scientist
source_jobs.description:
Company Description

At Autoscience Institute, we create AI systems that autonomously conduct AI research. Recently, we announced the first AI agent to autonomously create peer-reviewed literature (ICLR 2025 Workshops). We are passionate about pushing the boundaries of artificial intelligence and contributing to groundbreaking advancements in the field.

Role Description

This is a full-time on-site role for a Machine Learning Research Scientist located in the San Francisco Bay Area.

 - Work directly with the founder to develop autonomous research systems that ideate, experiment, and improve customer models.

 - Collaborate with the engineering team to build and deploy production-ready research systems.

 - RL post-train and fine-tune reasoning models to automate components of the machine learning research process.

 - Stay current with the latest developments in AI research and automation.

Qualifications:

 - Education: PhD or equivalent research experience in Computer Science, Machine Learning, Artificial Intelligence, or a related field. Exceptional candidates with strong research contributions are encouraged to apply regardless of formal degree.

 - Research: Publishing in top-tier AI/ML conferences (e.g., NeurIPS, ICML, ICLR, etc) or equivalent industry experience at corporate AI research labs (Microsoft, Google, Nvidia, TRI etc).

 - Technical: Expertise in training machine learning models, including deep learning, reinforcement learning or genetic algorithms. This does not include building multi-agent systems using LLM APIs or building RAG-based agents.

 - Curiosity: Passion for accelerating scientific discovery through AI and willingness to explore uncharted directions with minimal supervision.

Recommended Qualifications

 - Systems: Experience building scalable and production-ready machine learning pipelines or large-scale model training (distributed model training over >64 GPUs).

 - Science: Any background or proven interested in Automated Scientific Research is a plus.

Benefits & Perks

 - Our comp is competitive against major LLM frontier lab packages

 - Unlimited PTO and flexible working arrangements

 - Conference attendance and publication support

Our Culture

We're a team of passionate researchers and engineers working to automate scientific discovery. We believe in the power of AI to accelerate scientific discovery and are committed to responsible AI development

We are an e-verify employer.

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Company Description

At Autoscience Institute, we create AI systems that autonomously conduct AI research. Recently, we announced the first AI agent to autonomously create peer-reviewed literature (ICLR 2025 Workshops). We are passionate about pushing the boundaries of artificial intelligence and contributing to groundbreaking advancements in the field.

Role Description

This is a full-time on-site role for a Software Engineer located in the San Francisco Bay Area. The Software Engineer will be responsible for developing and maintaining back-end systems, designing and implementing software solutions, and creating production infrastructure. The role involves collaboration with our research teams to enhance our automated research capabilities.

Qualifications

 - Experience working in 24/7 production environments

 - Demonstrable experience with Google Cloud Platform

 - Demonstrable performance with Python

 - Strong skills in designing software (be ready to submit design docs)

 - Experience with backend and production environments

 - Ability to work collaboratively in a team environment and with Agile methods

 - Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Math, or a related field

 - Experience with AI and machine learning is a plus

Benefits & Perks

 - Our comp is competitive against major LLM frontier lab packages

 - Unlimited PTO and flexible working arrangements

 - Conference attendance and publication support

Our Culture

We're a team of passionate researchers and engineers working to automate scientific discovery. We believe in the power of AI to accelerate scientific discovery and are committed to responsible AI development.


We are an e-verify employer.

source_jobs.title: Senior / Staff Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Company Description

At Autoscience Institute, we automate AI research. Recently, we announced the first AI agent to autonomously create peer-reviewed literature (ICLR 2025) and the first AI system to win a Kaggle medal. We are passionate about pushing the boundaries of artificial intelligence and contributing to groundbreaking advancements in the field.

Role Description

This is a full-time on-site role for a Senior or Staff Software Engineer (Technical Lead) located in the San Francisco Bay Area.

In this role, you will lead the design and development of core systems that power autonomous research agents. You’ll be responsible for building reliable backend infrastructure and production systems that support large-scale AI experimentation and research workflows.

We are looking for engineers who have owned complex projects end-to-end, contributed to system architecture, and helped guide small teams of engineers through delivery. You should be comfortable writing high-quality code, producing clear design documents, and making thoughtful technical decisions that shape how systems evolve over time.

You will work closely with both engineers and researchers to translate experimental ideas into robust production systems.

Responsibilities

 - Lead the design and implementation of core backend and infrastructure systems

 - Own projects from architecture and design through production deployment

 - Write clear technical design documents and drive engineering decisions

 - Build and maintain 24/7 production systems supporting autonomous research agents

 - Partner closely with research teams to productionize experimental workflows

 - Guide and collaborate with engineers on system design and implementation

 - Improve system reliability, scalability, and observability

 - Help shape the technical direction of the platform

Qualifications

 - Experience leading complex engineering projects or technical initiatives

 - Experience acting as a technical lead or guiding small teams of engineers

 - Strong software design and architecture skills

 - Demonstrable experience writing clear technical design documents

 - Strong experience with Python

 - Experience building or operating backend or production infrastructure

 - Experience working in 24/7 production environments

 - Experience with Google Cloud Platform (GCP) or similar cloud infrastructure

 - Strong understanding of software evolution, maintainability, and system design tradeoffs

 - Ability to collaborate effectively with both engineers and researchers

 - Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Mathematics, or a related field

 - Strong candidates will typically have 5+ years of experience building and operating production software systems, including ownership of projects from design through deployment

Benefits & Perks

 - Our comp is competitive against major LLM frontier lab packages

 - Unlimited PTO and flexible working arrangements

 - Conference attendance and publication support

Our Culture

We're a team of passionate researchers and engineers working to automate scientific discovery. We believe in the power of AI to accelerate scientific discovery and are committed to responsible AI development.

source_jobs.title: Product Marketing Manager
source_jobs.description:
Company Description
At Autoscience Institute, we create AI systems that autonomously conduct AI research. Recently, we announced the first AI agent to autonomously create peer-reviewed literature (ICLR 2025 Workshops). We are passionate about pushing the boundaries of artificial intelligence and contributing to groundbreaking advancements in the field.

Role Description
This is a full-time on-site role for a Product Marketing Manager located in the San Francisco Bay Area. The Product Marketing Manager will be responsible for developing product positioning, messaging, go-to-market strategy, and sales enablement materials for Autoscience’s AI research systems. The role involves close collaboration with our research, engineering, sales, and leadership teams to communicate the value of our automated research capabilities to customers, partners, candidates, and the broader AI community.

Qualifications

 - Experience in product marketing, technical marketing, or go-to-market roles for AI, enterprise software, developer tools, or deep technology products

 - Demonstrable ability to translate complex technical products into clear and compelling positioning, messaging, and customer-facing materials

 - Strong writing and storytelling skills across product launches, website copy, sales collateral, case studies, blog posts, and executive communications

 - Experience developing go-to-market plans for new products, features, or market segments

 - Ability to work closely with technical teams, including researchers and engineers, to understand product capabilities and communicate differentiation

 - Strong customer and market research skills, including competitive analysis, customer interviews, and buyer persona development

 - Experience creating sales enablement materials such as pitch decks, one-pagers, battlecards, demo narratives, and objection-handling guides

 - Ability to work collaboratively in a team environment and with Agile methods

 - Experience with AI, machine learning, scientific software, or research workflows is a plus


Benefits & Perks

 - Our comp is competitive against major LLM frontier lab packages

 - Unlimited PTO and flexible working arrangements

 - Conference attendance and publication support
   



Our Culture
We’re a team of passionate researchers, engineers, and operators working to automate scientific discovery. We believe in the power of AI to accelerate scientific discovery and are committed to responsible AI development.

We are an e-verify employer.

source_jobs.title: Project Manager
source_jobs.description:
Company Description

At Autoscience Institute, we create AI systems that autonomously conduct AI research. Recently, we announced the first AI agent to autonomously create peer-reviewed literature (ICLR 2025 Workshops). We are passionate about pushing the boundaries of artificial intelligence and contributing to groundbreaking advancements in the field.

Role Description

This is a full time role for a Project Manager located in the San Francisco Bay Area, working on site.

With paying enterprise customers now on the platform, this role exists to keep our engineering and research teams shipping clean, reliable work on schedule. The person in this role needs to be technical enough to follow architecture discussions and design docs, but is focused on execution, not code. You'll be the person the team trusts to turn ambiguous priorities into a real plan and hold everyone accountable to it.

Responsibilities

 - Own program status across engineering and research workstreams, proactively identifying risks and unblocking progress before they threaten a milestone

 - Translate customer commitments into clear internal priorities and timelines

 - Act as a technical bridge between engineers, researchers, and customer facing stakeholders

 - Partner with engineering leads and researchers to turn ambiguous priorities into concrete, sequenced plans

 - Keep leadership informed on progress, risk, and delivery timelines

 - Instill a culture of shipping reliably: clean, tested, production ready work, on schedule

Qualifications

 - Experience as a Project Manager, Program Manager, or Technical Program Manager at a fast moving software or AI company

 - Comfort reading technical specs, architecture docs, or PRs well enough to follow the conversation

 - Track record of holding technical teams accountable to deadlines without being their manager

 - Strong organizational instincts and excellent communication skills across technical and non technical stakeholders

 - A natural ability to build trust and rapport with engineers and researchers

 - Comfort operating in ambiguity at an early stage startup with a small, fast moving team

 - Bias toward action and follow through

Recommended Qualifications

 - Experience supporting teams with live enterprise customers and real SLAs and commitments

 - Prior experience as a software engineer or in a hands on technical role before moving into program or project management

 - Familiarity with AI and ML product development cycles

Benefits & Perks

 - Our comp is competitive against major LLM frontier lab packages

 - Unlimited PTO and flexible working arrangements

 - Conference attendance and publication support

Our Culture
We’re a team of passionate researchers, engineers, and operators working to automate scientific discovery. We believe in the power of AI to accelerate scientific discovery and are committed to responsible AI development.

We are an e-verify employer.

notes: AI research-focused startup with strong engineering core
